leagueBriefing: The playoff races tightened on Monday night as Detroit used a massive second period to down Calgary, while Utah pulled off a statement win in Dallas. Elsewhere, Pittsburgh's offense exploded in Colorado, and the Devils stayed alive in the hunt with a dramatic overtime victory over the Bruins.

games.headline: Kane’s clinical finishing powers Red Wings past Flames
games.brief: Detroit turned a tight game into a rout with a four-goal second-period outburst. Patrick Kane showed he still has the magic touch, finding the back of the net twice to lead the Red Wings. Calgary struck first, but they simply couldn't handle Detroit's depth and efficiency once the Wings found their rhythm in front of the home crowd.
bullets: Patrick Kane notched two goals, marking his 4th multi-goal game of the season.
Alex DeBrincat provided the playmaking spark with 3 assists.
Detroit's four-goal second period matched their highest-scoring single period of the year.
The win moves Detroit five points clear in the Atlantic Division playoff race.
games.context: Detroit solidifies its hold on a top-three seed in the Atlantic Division.

highlights.title: Flames vs. Red Wings | NHL Highlights | March 16, 2026

games.headline: Cotter plays hero as Devils stun Bruins in overtime
games.brief: In a game that felt like a playoff preview, the Devils rallied from an early deficit to take down the Bruins in the extra frame. Paul Cotter was the man of the hour, scoring twice including the overtime winner. Despite a vintage performance from David Pastrnak, Boston couldn't close the door on a New Jersey team fighting for its postseason life.
bullets: Paul Cotter scored his first career NHL overtime winner.
David Pastrnak's two goals brought his season total to 45.
New Jersey erased a 2-0 first-period deficit to claim the win.
Devils goaltender made 32 saves to keep the Bruins at bay in the third period.
games.context: New Jersey remains within two points of the final Wild Card spot in the East.

highlights.title: Bruins vs. Devils | NHL Highlights | March 16, 2026

games.headline: Kings stifle Rangers in dominant road performance
games.brief: The Kings put on a defensive clinic at Madison Square Garden, frustrating the Rangers from the opening whistle. Alex Laferriere led the charge offensively with a three-point night, while the LA blue line limited New York to just a handful of high-danger chances. It was a professional road win for a Kings team looking to find consistency before April.
bullets: Alex Laferriere recorded a goal and two assists in his best performance of the month.
The Rangers were held scoreless until the final five minutes of the game.
Anze Kopitar moved into the top 50 all-time NHL scoring list with his assist.
LA has now won four of its last five road games.
games.context: The Kings move into a tie for second place in the Pacific Division.

highlights.title: Kings vs. Rangers | NHL Highlights | March 16, 2026

games.headline: Mammoth storm Dallas with four-goal third period
games.brief: Utah proved they belong among the Western Conference elite with a stunning comeback victory in Dallas. Trailing late in the game, the Mammoth exploded for four goals in the final period to shock the Stars. It was a display of depth and resilience that served notice to the rest of the league that Utah is a legitimate threat.
bullets: Utah's four goals in the third period came in a span of just 12 minutes.
Nate Schmidt provided the leadership on the back end and a key insurance goal.
Wyatt Johnston scored for Dallas, but the Stars' defense crumbled late.
This marks Utah's first win in Dallas in franchise history.
games.context: Utah closes the gap on Dallas for the top spot in the Central Division.

highlights.title: Mammoth vs. Stars | NHL Highlights | March 16, 2026

games.headline: Vintage Malkin leads Penguins' offensive barrage in Denver
games.brief: The Penguins turned back the clock in Colorado, putting up seven goals on one of the league's stingiest home teams. Evgeni Malkin was the orchestrator, bagging two goals and an assist in a dominant performance. Pittsburgh's power play finally clicked, and they caught the Avalanche on a rare off-night for their goaltending core.
bullets: Evgeni Malkin recorded his 15th career three-point game against Colorado.
Erik Karlsson added three points from the blue line (1G, 2A).
The seven goals conceded are the most the Avalanche have allowed at home this season.
Pittsburgh went 3-for-4 on the power play.
games.context: Pittsburgh stays in the thick of the Metropolitan Division playoff race.

highlights.title: Penguins vs. Avalanche | NHL Highlights | March 16, 2026
